title: Baroque Works Fans the Flames
description: On pages 13–16, this part focuses on baroque works fans the flames, keeping the scene centered on the conflict, choices, and stakes of the Alabasta arc.
scene: Baroque Works Fans the Flames. Vivi’s desperate attempts to stop the fighting are drowned out by the chaos Crocodile engineered. This section presents the immediate scene beat before the dialogue continues.

dialogue.speaker: Crocodile
dialogue.text: I told you before that I had no time to play with you any longer than that...

dialogue.speaker: Chaka
dialogue.text: Look! There's a sand- storm to the east!

dialogue.speaker: Pell
dialogue.text: Wait! The wind's blowing south so it should miss us!

dialogue.speaker: Koza
dialogue.text: A sandstorm just sprung up near Rainbow...!

dialogue.speaker: Unknown person #1
dialogue.text: Listen well, Straw-Eat Luffy. The prevailing winds here usually blow North to South.

dialogue.speaker: Unknown person #1
dialogue.text: So if a little sand- storm were to start here and head South while growing larger and larger from the prevailing winds,

dialogue.speaker: Vivi
dialogue.text: Where do you think it'll hit when it's grown into a monstrous sandstorm?

dialogue.speaker: Luffy
dialogue.text: Stop! Why would you do that!?

dialogue.speaker: Luffy
dialogue.text: Look, it's already begun to travel South...!

dialogue.speaker: Luffy
dialogue.text: The dried-up gramps has got nothing to do with our fight!

dialogue.speaker: Unknown person #2
dialogue.text: You bastard! Stop this at once!

summary: This part develops baroque works fans the flames and pushes the Alabasta conflict closer to its next decisive turn.
